channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Fired head of the agency that protects whistleblowers sues Trump, saying he was ousted illegally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The recently fired head of the Office of Special Counsel, a federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers, sued President Donald Trump on Monday, saying he was illegally ousted as part of the president’s massive overhaul of the government.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump has unleashed chaos by distraction upon the international community. That's no accident
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Saudis are furious. The Danes are scrambling. Colombia has backed down. Mexico and Canada stand in a purgatory between tariff wars with the US and … not. China has retaliated, launching a trade war between the economic superpowers.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Border Patrol 'dismantle' smuggling ring at El Paso hotel
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- Border Patrol agents in El Paso "dismantled" a human smuggling operation at a local hotel, Border Patrol Chief Michael W. Banks said on social media.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Trump sued after quietly firing top whistleblower protector
item.description: President Trump late Friday fired the leader of an office designed to protect government whistleblowers, a detail revealed in a Monday lawsuit from the head of the Office of the Special Counsel (OSC).
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Former Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel nominated as Ohio's lieutenant governor
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine on Monday nominated former Ohio State Buckeyes football coach Jim Tressel to be the state's next lieutenant governor. Tressel retired a year and a half ago as president of Youngstown State University, a job he had held since 2014.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kendrick Lamar and SZA are expanding their tour to Europe
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fresh off their Super Bowl halftime spectacle, Kendrick Lamar and SZA are expanding their upcoming tour to make stops in Europe. The additional stops announced Monday include a July 4 show in Frankfurt, Germany.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump says he has directed US Treasury to stop minting new pennies, citing rising cost
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin. “For far too long the United States has minted pennies which literally cost us more than 2 cents.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blue state Democrats spearhead bills to crack down on campus antisemitism
item.description: More than a year after the Oct. 7 Hamas terror attack on Israel sparked U.S. campus protests, New York lawmakers propose a crackdown on hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Super Bowl halftime performer detained after unfurling Sudanese-Palestinian flag
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A performer in Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show was detained on the field and could face charges after unfurling a combination Sudanese-Palestinian flag with “Sudan” and “Gaza” written on it. The NFL confirmed the person was part of the 400-member field cast.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What happens to the Super Bowl losers' merch? It's being sent to these 5 countries
item.description: The NFL plans for both outcomes so they're ready regardless of who wins. What that inevitably means is a whole lot of merchandise that's pre-printed and pre-made – but fans won't ever be able to buy it.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: USPS employee sentenced to prison after stealing $24 million worth of checks: officials
item.description: Former U.S. Postal Service employee Nakedra Shannon was sentenced to prison after she and co-conspirators stole over $24 million worth of mailed checks, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Court grants request to block detained Venezuelan immigrants from being sent to Guantanamo
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A federal court on Sunday blocked the Trump administration from sending three Venezuelan immigrants held in New Mexico to Guantanamo Bay Naval Base in Cuba as part of the president's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are focusing on the wreckage of a plane that crashed off western Alaska as they try to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea, killing 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance and Musk question the authority of the courts as Trump's agenda faces legal pushback
item.description: Top Trump administration officials are openly questioning the judiciary’s authority to serve as a check on executive power as the new president’s sweeping agenda faces growing pushback from the courts.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Los Angeles wildfire czar's $500K paycheck for 90 days of work draws swift blowback, mayor reverses course
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass reneged wildfire czar Steve Soboroff's $500,000 compensation for 90 days of work after the amount drew criticism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ivy League grad student, US Army veteran's killer had eerie motive: detective
item.description: A Yale graduate student and U.S. army veteran was killed in what initially appeared to be a random act of violence, and a detective on the case reveals how the killer was nabbed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Noem responds to Secret Service scrutiny as Trump to become 1st president to attend Super Bowl
item.description: DHS Secretary Kristi Noem said Secret Service would not be the only federal agency tasked with defending the Super Bowl during President Donald Trump's visit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Putting carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where it won’t contribute to global warming could see less federal support and enthusiasm under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE arrests illegal migrant accused of entering the country 6 times
item.description: Jorge Carvajal Castrejon, 36, an illegal migrant, was arrested Jan. 28 in Houston after allegedly entering the US six times, during an enhanced ICE operation with partners from the FBI and other agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Iowa's farm country, small towns rally around a native son in football's biggest game
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — In the farm town where Cooper DeJean drew up football plays in elementary school and taught classmates to run them at recess, residents plan a huge party to watch the town’s native son in the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4

item.title: Family files lawsuit after mother, son die from food poisoning while on vacation
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KRON4

item.title: Stage and film actor Tony Roberts, who often starred in Woody Allen movies, dies at 85
item.description: Tony Roberts, a versatile, Tony Award-nominated theater performer at home in both plays and musicals and who appeared in several Woody Allen movies — often as Allen's best friend — has died. He was 85.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana woman arrested following death of Kansas City reporter covering Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Louisiana woman has been arrested after she was allegedly caught using the reporter's credit cards in the New Orleans area.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, WGN 9

item.title: Dog Show 101: What's what at the Westminster Kennel Club
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Westminster Kennel Club dog show is back for a 149th year — and back at New York's Madison Square Garden for the first time since early 2020.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alabama puts man to death for a 1991 murder in the nation's fourth execution using nitrogen gas
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An man convicted of murdering a woman after breaking into her apartment as she slept was put to death Thursday evening in Alabama in the nation’s fourth execution using nitrogen gas. Demetrius Frazier, 52, was pronounced dead at 6:36 p.m.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames 'obsolete' US air traffic control system for the plane and chopper collision near DC
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday blamed the deadly collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter last week on what he called an “obsolete” computer system used by U.S. air traffic controllers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4
